error_signature: duplicate key value violates unique constraint
title: Duplicate key value error in PostgreSQL node
symptom: When trying to insert data into a PostgreSQL table, n8n throws a 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint' error, indicating that the combination of specified keys already exists.
applies_when: Inserting multiple items into a table with unique constraints
not_this_if: Inserting a single item that does not violate constraints
root_cause: The error occurs because the workflow is attempting to insert multiple items that contain duplicate values for the unique constraint defined on the table. Each execution of the node for each item can lead to attempts to insert the same key combination, resulting in this error.
fix_steps: Check the number of items being sent to the PostgreSQL node.
Ensure that each item represents a unique row to be inserted.
If sending multiple items, verify that they do not contain duplicate values for the unique constraint.
agent_instruction: Ensure that the data being sent to the PostgreSQL node does not contain duplicates for the unique constraint.
